_meta.title: CR new-news — Nearly $90M of outside money is picking the next Texas senator (TX Senate 2026 super-PACs)
_meta.purpose: Friendly PAC names are a costume; the public FEC filing is where the real face is written. B-ROLL-FIRST: money facts → Remotion, named donors/candidates → politician-caricature, atmosphere → fal text-free plates. Real candidate soundbites (Paxton/Allred/Crockett/Cornyn) added at Stage 18 cinematic-broll layer.

beats.label: Receipt 1 — Texans for a Conservative Majority

clips.covers_script_section: HOOK — tired of these ads, picture yourself on the couch
clips.source_citation: Atmosphere only — Higgsfield/fal text-free plate generated at Stage 21 (prompt in -prompts.json). No claim on screen.

clips.covers_script_section: Nearly ninety million dollars is already picking your next senator

props.label: Outside money in one Texas Senate race
props.caption: Independent committees — not the candidates' campaigns · FEC Schedule E, S/TX/2026

clips.covers_script_section: It says it is from Texans for a Conservative Majority. Who actually paid for that ad?

props.text: On the ad it says “Texans.” Who paid?

clips.covers_script_section: Stakes — the most expensive Senate race in the country

props.text: One Texas U.S. Senate seat. The most expensive Senate race in the country.

clips.covers_script_section: What is a super PAC — raises huge checks, buys ads, can't hand money to a campaign

destinations.label: Cannot give to a campaign
destinations.outcome: So the name is the group, not the candidate

clips.covers_script_section: The race for one Texas seat in the U.S. Senate
clips.source_citation: Atmosphere only — Higgsfield/fal text-free establishing plate generated at Stage 21 (prompt in -prompts.json).

clips.covers_script_section: Start with the biggest one — Texans for a Conservative Majority

props.citation: FEC.gov · Committee C00542217 · Texans for a Conservative Majority
props.quote: Every super-PAC must tell the government who pays it. That filing is public. It is free to read.

clips.covers_script_section: It is mostly one man — John Nau, Texas beer-distribution billionaire, ~$15.6M
A Wall Street boss named Stephen Schwarzman added four million
That group has spent twenty-four million, all against one Republican, Ken Paxton

props.title: Texans for a Conservative Majority — who really paid

destinations.label: All of it AGAINST Ken Paxton

clips.covers_script_section: All of it against one Republican, Ken Paxton
Truth and Courage — biggest name we can see is Jeff Yass at two million
About twenty-two million, all against the Democrat, Colin Allred

props.title: Truth and Courage — the biggest name we can see

destinations.label: Jeff Yass · $2M itemized
destinations.outcome: Biggest NAMED donor we can see

destinations.label: All of it AGAINST Colin Allred

clips.covers_script_section: All of it against the Democrat, Colin Allred
Lone Star Rising — backer is California tech billionaire Reid Hoffman, $1.5M
Liberal money against a Democrat, Jasmine Crockett — both sides wear the costume

props.title: Lone Star Rising — sounds Texan, the money is liberal

destinations.label: All of it AGAINST Jasmine Crockett

clips.covers_script_section: And it spends against a Democrat, Jasmine Crockett
Paxton and Allred want to speak for thirty million Texans

props.text: These candidates want to speak for 30 million Texans.

clips.covers_script_section: One voter gets one ballot; a billionaire gets fifteen million dollars of airtime

props.title: Whose voice is louder?
props.subtitle: One ballot vs. one billionaire's airtime

rows.name: A Texas voter
rows.policy: Drives across town after work to vote

clips.covers_script_section: Money you were never meant to trace

props.text: A handful of billionaires. Not a crowd of Texans.

clips.covers_script_section: WinSenate $10M for Allred but donors not named; ~$5M against Wesley Hunt via one pass-through

props.title: Some threads do not pull all the way

clips.covers_script_section: We do not know those names yet. The filing does not show them. We will not make a name up.

props.quote: Donors NOT ITEMIZED in the 2026 filings. We will not make a name up.

clips.covers_script_section: The friendly names are a costume; the real face is in the public filing

props.text: The friendly name is the costume. Not the message.

clips.covers_script_section: The real face is written in only one place — the public filing

props.promise: THE WARM NAME IS THE DISGUISE.
props.citation: FEC.gov · Schedule E + Schedule A · TX Senate 2026 · retrieved May 2026

clips.covers_script_section: Cold close — it is one filing, it is free, it is already yours. Newsletter linked below.

props.cta: Read the receipt. It is already yours.

music_cues.mood: single unresolved felt-piano figure, one held minor note, a question hanging
music_cues.instrumentation: solo felt piano, no pad, no drums
music_cues.prompt: Solo felt piano, slow 58 bpm, a single unresolved minor figure repeating sparsely with long silence between phrases. Documentary cold open, a question left hanging in the air. No drums, no pad, no swell. Frontline first-20-seconds restraint.

music_cues.mood: steady investigative pulse, low cello + muted upright bass, patient forward motion
music_cues.instrumentation: felt piano, low cello, soft muted upright bass, light pizzicato accents
music_cues.prompt: Patient investigative documentary underscore, 70 bpm, felt piano with low cello and a soft muted upright bass walking quietly underneath, occasional pizzicato accent as each fact lands. Restrained, archival, building trust. No drums, no big swell, no tension yet.

music_cues.mood: slow sinister rise, low drone swelling under the pattern, dissonant cello, ticking unease
music_cues.instrumentation: low synth drone, dissonant cello, sparse ticking percussion, piano lower register
music_cues.prompt: Slowly rising sinister documentary tension, 72 bpm, a low drone swelling underneath a dissonant cello line, a sparse ticking pulse like a clock, piano playing in its lower register. The unease grows steadily as the same trick repeats across both parties. No release yet — hold the tension. No melody resolves.

music_cues.mood: the tension resolves — a single clarifying major chord opens up, light breaks through
music_cues.instrumentation: warm piano major resolution, a single sustained string swell up, soft bell shimmer
music_cues.prompt: The held tension finally resolves — a warm major piano chord opens up with a single sustained string swell rising into clarity and a soft bell shimmer on top. Cinematic ta-da release, the moment the disguise comes off and the truth is plain. 70 bpm, hopeful and clean. No drums.

music_cues.mood: resolved, grounded, certain — the verdict settles
music_cues.instrumentation: warm piano, sustained low strings, steady and final
music_cues.prompt: Grounded, certain documentary resolution, 68 bpm, warm piano over sustained low strings, steady and final, the verdict settling into place. Confident but not triumphant. No drums.

music_cues.mood: warm, dignified, leaves the viewer with agency
music_cues.instrumentation: solo felt piano returns, one gentle resolved figure, soft string bed underneath
music_cues.prompt: Warm, dignified close, 60 bpm, solo felt piano playing one gentle resolved figure over a soft low string bed, leaving the viewer with quiet agency — the receipt is already yours. Mirrors the cold-open piano but now resolved. Fade to silence on a 1.2s tail. No drums.

sfx.cue: soft mechanical odometer ticking that accelerates and lands on a low thud as the $88.6M number settles
single crisp paper-flip / filing page turn as the FEC filing is pulled
soft pen-on-paper scratch as each donor line is written into the money-flow
second crisp paper-flip — pulling the next thread
third paper-flip — and it happens again
soft cinematic riser resolving into a single warm bell hit exactly as the costume punchline lands
firm ink-stamp / gavel thud as the RECEIPT verdict stamp lands
